commentary_text: The market assigns Algeria a 61% win probability versus the model's 44%, a 17-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. The model sees materially closer odds across all three outcomes, whereas sportsbooks and prediction markets have sharpened conviction around an Algeria victory. This divergence likely reflects information available to real-money markets but not the model's training data—recent form, injury status, or lineup changes could all shift expectations toward Algeria without altering the fundamental Elo balance. The dashboard flags this as a signal worth monitoring: either the market has detected concrete new information tightening the race, or both sources are simply calibrated differently on the same underlying matchup.
